revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白山神社 (平群町)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hakusan Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:白山神社の境内.jpg | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}} {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri Town|1=Heguri, Nara}} Fukui 1551 | ISO = JP-29 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 37 | LatitudeSeconds = 52.6 | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 41 | LongitudeSeconds = 29.8 | Deities = {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} | Structure = | History = | MainHall = | HeadPriest = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = | Rituals = | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Heguri|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.631278|N|135.691611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Izanagi|qid=Q813858|1=Izanagi}}; {{ill|Izanami|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}} }} [[File:白山神社の拝殿の前.jpg|thumb|Front of the Worship Hall]] '''Hakusan Shrine''' (白山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri Town|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The shrine's former status under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a Village Shrine (村社).
== Description == The shrine was established on the site of Sanmyōin, a former sub-temple of the temple Jisonzan {{ill|Fukui-ji (Nara Prefecture)|ja|普門院 (奈良県平群町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukui-ji (Nara Prefecture)|1=Fumon-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135258022}}, which was built by the Heian-period monk Dōsen and served as a {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} shrine temple<ref>Whiteboard at Hakusan Shrine.</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=白山神社 (はくさんじんじゃ)｜へぐり散策マップ｜平群町観光 オフィシャルホームページ |url=http://kanko.town.heguri.nara.jp/walk/spot/spot76.php |website=Heguri Town Official Tourism Website |access-date=2022-09-18}}</ref>.
After the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the early Meiji period, it became the guardian shrine for the Fukui district and {{ill|Fukui-ji (Nara Prefecture)|ja|普門院 (奈良県平群町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukui-ji (Nara Prefecture)|1=Fumon-in|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q135258022}}.
The founding date is unknown, but it is believed to be linked to {{ill|Hakusan Faith|lt=Hakusan Faith|lt=Hakusan worship|ja|白山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan worship|qq=}} and may have been established during the Heian period<ref>{{Cite kotobank|word=白山信仰|accessdate=2022-09-22}}</ref>. The shrine is located eastward near the foot of a hill west of the local settlement<ref name="heguri1976">{{Cite book|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=Heguri Town History|publisher=Heguri Town Office|year=1976|pages=652-653}}</ref>.
== Structure == Passing through the second torii and ascending the stone steps, one finds a partitioned worship hall on the right. Beyond this stands the main hall, built in a {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} style with a cypress bark roof, enclosed by a protective structure to shield it from the elements.
The shrine enshrines {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}. The 明細帳 (Meiji 25) describes the shrine as worshipping "Hakusan Ōkami (白山大神)" in some accounts.
The main hall also houses a kettle inscribed with "Jisonzan Hakusan Daimyōjin" and "Kyōhō 14" (1729), stored in the temple's warehouse<ref>{{Cite web |title=Hakusan Shrine |url=http://www.komainu.org/nara/ikomagunn_heguri/hakusan/hakusan.html |website=www.komainu.org |access-date=2022-09-18}}</ref>.
== Surroundings == The precinct includes the following structures: * {{ill|Mirokudō (Maitreya Hall)|ja|弥勒堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mirokudō (Maitreya Hall)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504618|1=Mirokudō (Maitreya Hall)}} * Associated Shrines: ** {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} (deity: {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}) ** {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|淡嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (deity: {{ill|Awashima Daimyōjin|ja|淡嶋大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504622|1=Awashima Daimyōjin}})
Behind the Mirokudō, a climb of about 10 meters leads to a memorial grave for the monk Dōsen (deceased {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} 18, October 2)<ref>{{Cite web|title=道詮律師墓 (どうせんりっしはか)｜へぐり散策マップ｜平群町観光 オフィシャルホームページ |url=http://kanko.town.heguri.nara.jp/walk/spot/spot46.php |website=Heguri Town Official Tourism Website |access-date=2022-09-18}}</ref>.
Additionally, a small shrine 6 meters before the main shrine houses a {{ill|Yin Stone|ja|陰石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Stone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>A stone representing female anatomy.</ref>.
The shrine also holds {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|qid=Q3080781|lt=Honji Buddha|1=Honji suijaku}}, such as wooden statues of the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|qid=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}}, {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}}, and {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|qid=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu|1=Kṣitigarbha}}, attributed to the Muromachi period, which are now housed at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|qid=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum|1=Nara National Museum}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=Heguri Town History Editorial Committee|title=Heguri Town History|publisher=Heguri Town Office|year=1976|ncid=BN04492478}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Hakusan-jinja_(Heguri)}} * [https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0663.html Hakusan Shrine (Heguri Town)]
{{Shinto Horizontal}}
{{translated page|ja|白山神社 (平群町)|version=103083451|comment=Imported full ja history}}
